extraction.one_line_summary: CAVENDISH Mark won final stage sprint in Rome with late-race positioning after MILAN Jonathan's leadout, securing first Astana win in 4 years at Giro
tactical_patterns: THOMAS Geraint pulled leadout work for CAVENDISH in closing kilometers, suggesting team tactics shifted post-GC finalization on Stage 20.
MILAN Jonathan's leadout failed despite winning overall points classification, exposing vulnerability in Bahrain's sprint train execution under pressure.
Breakaway caught with 13km remaining (one lap to go) indicates Rome circuit's profile favors sprinters holding back until final lap.

rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: positive: came off MILAN's wheel rather than relying on own leadout, showing tactical opportunism in Roman circuit sprint
rider_signals.MILAN Jonathan: negative: won points jersey but failed own sprint after providing leadout, questioning dual-role capacity in high-pressure finales
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: neutral: working for CAVENDISH post-GC suggests Ineos tactical flexibility when overall objectives secured
